goal: Add a side-effect-free readiness gate for applying BPK-065 runtime server patch candidates, requiring operator, diff, and execution-closed refs while keeping execution, patch application, server mutation, route mount, orchestrator, providers, DB, GitHub, writes, packages, workflows, and deployment out of scope.
scope_out: Do not apply patches, mount routes, call orchestrator, call providers, call DB, call GitHub, write files, deploy, change workflows, change packages, or enable execution.
claims: Runtime server patch application readiness can be represented without applying the patch.
Ready BPK-065 candidate plus operator, diff, and execution-closed refs are required.
Execution and patch application remain false.
assumptions: Actual runtime patch application requires a later task lock.
dod: Ready, missing execution-closed ref, and blocked candidate cases are tested.
Focused test, typecheck, task-lock verify, and diff-check pass.
stop_conditions: Route mount, execution, orchestrator, provider, DB, GitHub, write, deployment, package, or workflow changes become necessary.
prior_task_findings: BPK-065 represented runtime server patch candidates without applying them.
